Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Megadev: A Development Kit for the Sega Mega Drive and Mega CD Hardware 91 upvotes, 5 comments
  2. Baochip-1x: What it is, why I'm doing it now and how it came about 214 upvotes, 26 comments
  3. Show HN: GitAgent – An open standard that turns any Git repo into an AI agent 36 upvotes, 2 comments
  4. Can I run AI locally? 1361 upvotes, 322 comments
  5. NMAP in the Movies 80 upvotes, 12 comments
  6. Sunsetting Jazzband 24 upvotes, 2 comments
  7. Secure Secrets Management for Cursor Cloud Agents 34 upvotes, 5 comments
  8. Cookie jars capture American kitsch (2023) 16 upvotes, 1 comments
  9. I found 39 Algolia admin keys exposed across open source documentation sites 150 upvotes, 44 comments
  10. Python: The Optimization Ladder 180 upvotes, 55 comments
  11. Show HN: Ink – Deploy full-stack apps from AI agents via MCP or Skills 21 upvotes, 3 comments
  12. Atari 2600 BASIC Programming (2015) 51 upvotes, 13 comments
  13. 1M context is now generally available for Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 1045 upvotes, 439 comments
  14. Philosoph Jürgen Habermas Gestorben 105 upvotes, 36 comments
  15. The Isolation Trap: Erlang 125 upvotes, 51 comments
  16. Online astroturfing: A problem beyond disinformation 48 upvotes, 18 comments
  17. Everything you never wanted to know about visually-hidden 15 upvotes, 3 comments
  18. Show HN: Learn Arabic with spaced repetition and comprehensible input 12 upvotes, 2 comments
  19. A Survival Guide to a PhD (2016) 155 upvotes, 93 comments
  20. What happens when US economic data becomes unreliable 153 upvotes, 99 comments
  21. The $2 per hour worker behind the OnlyFans boom 38 upvotes, 19 comments
  22. Montana passes Right to Compute act (2025) 171 upvotes, 121 comments
  23. RAM kits are now sold with one fake RAM stick alongside a real one 189 upvotes, 136 comments
  24. Nominal Types in WebAssembly 27 upvotes, 13 comments
  25. An Ode to Bzip 31 upvotes, 16 comments
  26. UBI Is Your Productivity Dividend – The Only Way to All Share What We All Built 59 upvotes, 46 comments
  27. Digg is gone again 340 upvotes, 350 comments
  28. XML Is a Cheap DSL 189 upvotes, 194 comments
  29. Wired headphone sales are exploding 347 upvotes, 580 comments
  30. 9 Mothers Defense (YC P26) Is Hiring in Austin 0 upvotes, 0 comments
